title: rubus rivularis var. helveconicus
intro: Rubus rivularis var. helveconicus, also known as the Himalayan raspberry, is a variety of the Himalayan raspberry plant, a type of bramble with edible fruits and leaves. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal issues. The leaves and roots of Rubus rivularis var. helveconicus contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, which are believed to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving cardiovascular health. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in various forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used in traditional medicine to support overall well-being.
